text:
The purpose of this study is to investigate ways and means of integrating philosophical ethics and prophetic values in the Islamic educational system in order to establish a humanistic, just and innovative educational paradigm. While philosophical ethics depend on human reason and intellect, and accordingly, exercise critical thinking, prophetic values are based on Islamic teachings. Therefore, by integrating philosophical ethics and prophetic values in the Islamic educational system, it is possible to establish a comprehensive educational program that cultivates human virtues, social responsibility, spiritual awareness and critical thinking.

The present study utilizes qualitative research methodology of literature review and content analysis. The objective of this study is to identify and examine the relations and interactions between the philosophical and prophetic ethics as well as to clarify their similarities and differences. According to the results of the research, both philosophical and prophetic ethics agree on the concepts of good, truth and humanity, however the sources of their theoretical bases are different. While the source of philosophical ethics is based on social norms and human reason, that of prophetic ethics is based on the verses of the holy Quran and the Hadith.

The integrated model propose a humanistic approach that honors human dignity, social justice that ensures equity and fairness in dealing with humans, and an empowering vision that turns students into agents of change. Thus, the integration of the model’s components works towards achieving a society that boasts learners of high academic achievement and strong ethical values grounded on the Islamic values.
